old_issue: Generic fantasy labels and placeholder names.
why_it_fits: Stops names sounding plastic and gives chronicles multiple perspectives.

old_issue: Magic risked becoming flavour or a shortcut.
upgrade: Converted mana into capacity/control/recovery/strain/legal status with costs and limits.
why_it_fits: Lets magic affect history without deleting logistics, disease, debt or food.

old_issue: Fantasy species can become stereotypes or factions.
upgrade: Separated species, culture, state and religion into different CSV concepts.
why_it_fits: Allows orcs, elves, dwarves and humans to exist across cultures, classes and states.

old_issue: Lore text was not directly usable by the engine.
upgrade: Added mechanic hooks, AI weights, schemas, modifiers and seed-region data.
why_it_fits: Turns the lore bible into data the simulator can consume.

old_issue: The whole world was too broad to test at once.
upgrade: Added focused Rov Basin north-east frontier seed.
why_it_fits: Tests roads, grain, raids, mage law, species law, debt, claims and religion in one pressure zone.
